name: Mrs. King's Windemere Clam Chowder
recipeIngredient: 3 strips of bacon rendered until crisp
1 Dry and crumbled very fine (e.g., paprika)
recipeInstructions:
1. Open 11 cans of clams. Save the 12th can for blending with thickening ingredients. Pour the clam juice into a large cooking pot.
2. Add potatoes and onions to the clam juice. Cover and simmer until the potatoes are tender. From this point on, cook in an uncovered pot over low or medium heat. Never allow the chowder to boil!
3. Add clams, Worcestershire sauce, and optional bacon pieces.
4. In a blender, mix thoroughly 2 cups of milk, the reserved can of clams and its juice, flour, heavy cream, horse radish, and eggs.
5. Slowly stir the blended mixture into the cooking clams. Add the remaining 2 cups of milk. Turn heat down to low.
6. Cook for 10-15 minutes; stir occasionally, but do not allow the contents to boil.
7. Dissolve baking soda in one teaspoon of water and add it to the chowder to prevent ingredient separation.
8. Taste and season as necessary.
9. Float a stick of butter or margarine on top of the chowder. When melted, fold it in. Allow the chowder to rest for at least 3 hours before serving.
